item.title: Let’s not miss the huge opportunity for peace between Lebanon and Israel
item.description: Washington has a second chance to do the right thing.

item.title: Reliving a colonial, exploitative history in Venezuela and Iran
item.description: These two oil-rich nations have not forgotten the lessons from decades of unfair extraction. Did the U.S. learn from decades of subsequent conflict?

item.title: The stars align for Democrats in Texas. Trump is helping them
item.description: By failing to swiftly endorse for Senate, the president is setting up the two remaining Republican primary candidates for a fight that hurts the GOP.

item.title: Why healthcare is so expensive in America, and what to do about it
item.description: People are questioning if the architecture of American healthcare can create any living, breathing incentive for affordability.

item.title: Trump's war in Iran is already hurting him at home
item.description: In Congress, members of both parties are again trying to stop the president's illegal and unpopular war-making, as they did in 2019 to end his bombings in Yemen.

item.title: Can women push the L.A. Marathon into the same league as NYC and Boston?
item.description: With the culture of women's running exploding and the Olympics coming back in 2028, the marathon could turn into a big opportunity for the city.

item.title: On Iran, Russia and China, Trump's weakness for strongmen explains his foreign policy
item.description: There is no Trump doctrine because this president is highly suggestible by authoritarians such as Vladimir Putin and Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: U.S. attack on Iran echoes Russia's invasion of Ukraine
item.description: It's disturbing for the U.S. to be playing the role of the aggressor. Equally disturbing is to see mistakes of the Iraq War already being repeated.

item.title: Trump may not know what he wants or why he started this war
item.description: Regime change? Leverage? Supporting dissidents? Each points to a different path to ending the Iran war, and the White House has been muddled.

item.title: The Epstein files are off the front page, but far from yesterday's news
item.description: The repercussions from Jeffrey Epstein's decades-long criminal enterprise will continue to be felt for a long, long time.

item.title: Trump needs to get ready for the blowback
item.description: Everything unfolding in Iran depends on the consequences of one man’s unilateral decision to launch a war.

item.title: Gavin Newsom is a lot more complicated than you think
item.description: Son of a financially strapped mother and a father who was practically a Getty, Newsom explores his past in his new memoir, 'Young Man in a Hurry.'

item.title: There are two Americas. Falling mortgage rates matter only to the wealthy one
item.description: Protecting democracy requires making sure people feel that democracy is working for them.

item.title: To understand homelessness, listen to homeless people. Here's what I learned
item.description: Don't we all want better for all people who are this impoverished? Los Angeles cannot thrive if there is one community of people safely in homes and another forced onto the sidewalks.

item.title: Stop finding ways to kill the Venice Dell homeless housing project and get it built instead
item.description: It's outrageous that the city is still looking for ways to stop the project, years after it was approved.

item.title: For California towns with a bear problem, using dogs to hunt is no solution
item.description: Hounding bears won't prevent dangerous encounters with humans. But bear-proofing your property will help.

item.title: Keeping at-risk residents from losing their housing will be a key to solving homelessness
item.description: It is easier and less costly to keep someone from becoming homeless than it is to help them out of the trauma of homelessness and find them permanent housing.

item.title: New fire prevention bills are a science-based step in the right direction
item.description: 'For years, we’ve poured money into backcountry fuel projects that look like action but don’t stop wind‑whipped embers from turning neighborhoods into disaster zones. This new legislative package is a step toward correcting that mistake,' writes an L.A. Times reader.

item.title: A free market inherently doesn’t work when it comes to healthcare
item.description: 'Providers are required by law to treat patients, and sick or injured people are hardly in a position to walk away if the treatment is a little more costly than they would like,' writes an L.A. Times reader.

item.title: The spraying of herbicides in O.C. creeks is ‘dangerous and unnecessary’
item.description: 'Surely Orange County Public Works officials could use what officials in San Diego County are using: human beings with equipment to manually manage plant growth and removal,' writes an L.A. Times reader.
